concept: Wait / Remain in Readiness / Stay for What Comes
description: To wait: for a friend, for rain, for the bread to rise. Remaining present while time does its work. Kin to 'hurao' (patient, the quality) and 'manolu' (stay, the location) — waiting is staying with attention still on.
sound_symbolism: The quiet 'h' is held breath, the open 'a' is duration itself, and 'si' continues — the same syllable that marks ongoing aspect, fittingly, since waiting is pure ongoingness.
grammatical_notes: Intransitive: 'lo mia hasi.' (We wait.) With the awaited marked by 'wei': 'mia wei thia hasi.' (I wait for you.) With duration: 'shia sui howai to hasi.' (They waited through the evening.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Waiting is the layperson's meditation: presence without action, attention without object. A culture's relationship to waiting is its relationship to time.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Bread, seeds, seasons, births — the old crafts are mostly skilled waiting. Hurry was the industrial invention.
